Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

This patch is failing ost-pools.sh test_6 and test_20:

 ost-pools test_20: @@@@@@ FAIL: dir '/mnt/lustre/d20.ost-pools/dir1/dir3' not in any pool 
  Trace dump:
  = /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:4819:error()
  = /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/ost-pools.sh:103:check_dir_in_pool()
  = /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/ost-pools.sh:1195:test_20()

It doesn't appear to be caused by the changes to the tests, but rather the change to lod_qos.c itself, since this failure is early on in each test:

    $SETSTRIPE -c -1 -p $POOL $POOL_DIR
    [[ $? -eq 0 ]] || error "$SETSTRIPE -p $POOL failed."
    check_dir_in_pool $POOL_DIR $POOL

Patch Set 4:

With quick look, it seems that we are failing for the checks in the following "if" : (both test 6 and 20)

if [ $(lustre_version_code mds1) -ge $(version_code 2.8.54) ]; then

So trying to understand reference to that check

Patch Set 4:

Rahul - The server version check was put in to test pool inheritance for versions of Lustre that contained your patch.

Patch Set 4:

> Rahul - The server version check was put in to test pool
 > inheritance for versions of Lustre that contained your patch.

Apologies for delay in response here. I have figured out the problem. The problem is that when we do setstripe on directory (under the directory which is part of pool) and do not mention the pool name, in that case we over-write the no name with existing name. (or we allocate lov_user_md_v1 instead of lov_user_md_v3) Hence we lost the pool name. Cooked up the patch to retain the pool name. I hope the approach is correct and did not miss any thing, Please review.

parents.subject: LU-10514 utils: statically link l_getidentity with libcfs.a

commit.subject: LU-8264 lod: lfs setstripe fix for pool.
commit.message:
LU-8264 lod: lfs setstripe fix for pool.

If a file is created (with lfs) in the directory associated
with pool without -p pool_name option then limit stripe count
to number of osts in the pool as that directory is associated
with the pool. This patch fixes this problem.

Also removed the wrong check from ost-pools.sh, test_20 where
we were creating file in a directory associated with pool and
checking it as not part of the pool.

Add test cases in ost_pool.sh test_20.

Signed-off-by: Rahul Deshmukh <rahul.deshmukh@seagate.com>
Signed-off-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao.zhang@intel.com>
Seagate-bug-id: MRP-3615
Change-Id: Id6dd5126856db7fc773a1fe9c837a214db8d6d70
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/20849
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
